question.text: What was the role of the L.C. Gupta Committee in the development of derivatives trading in India?

answer.text: It was set up by SEBI to develop the regulatory framework for derivatives trading — it recommended eligibility criteria for exchanges, clearing corporations, and members
It was set up by RBI to design currency derivative products for Indian exchanges
It was a joint SEBI-RBI committee that designed the SPAN margining system
It was established by the Ministry of Finance to determine tax treatment of derivatives

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI constituted the L.C. Gupta Committee in 1998 to recommend the regulatory framework for derivatives trading. Key recommendations: separate governing council with max 40% trading member representation, minimum 50 exchange members, clearing through SEBI-approved CC, Rs. 3 crore minimum net worth for CMs, mandatory NISM certification, and strict KYC enforcement. **The Trap:** The L.C. Gupta Committee was a SEBI committee, not RBI. The J.R. Verma Committee (separate) worked on risk management and margining — they are often confused. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch8 covers L.C. Gupta Committee in detail. Series IV Ch8 mentions the RBI-SEBI Standing Technical Committee (different from L.C. Gupta). Series XIII tests understanding of different committees. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** L.C. Gupta = regulatory framework. J.R. Verma = risk management/margining. RBI-SEBI STC = currency and IR derivatives coordination. Know which committee did what.
